name: Saddle of Rabbit with Leeks and Rosemary
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Rabbit (use only the saddle section)
recipeInstructions:
-------------
LEEKS
2 medium Leeks
Salt (to taste)
1 tablespoon Butter
Saddle of a rabbit is the rabbit's back from the first rib to the beginning of the legs.
Marinade:
---------
Place the saddle of the rabbit in a small bowl with 1 cup white wine and 1 sprig of rosemary. Chop the carrot, onion, celery, and garlic; add them to the marinade. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 24 - 48 hours (longer enhances flavor).
Rabbit:
-------
Preheat oven to 450°F.
Remove rabbit from marinade and dry with a cloth. Salt and pepper the rabbit.
In a medium roasting pan, add 1 tablespoon butter and 1 tablespoon olive oil. Heat until butter sizzles.
Place rabbit skin side up in the pan; brown, wrapping flaps around the filet to protect it. Cook until lightly browned. Transfer pan to oven and cook 10 minutes (medium rare). Baste often.
Add marinade vegetables to the pan with rabbit; cook for another 5 minutes, basting and turning occasionally. Remove rabbit and set aside in a warm place.
Trim saddle flaps from rabbit; chop coarsely.
Sauce:
------
Return chopped flaps to pan with vegetables. Saute briefly, skimming fat.
Deglaze pan with 1/2 cup wine; add rabbit stock and reduce by half.
Add tomato; saute while stirring. Strain through sieve into saucepan, discarding solids. Add butter to liquid and whisk until smooth.
Leeks:
------
Julienne leeks. Rinse in cold water and drain well.
In a medium saute pan, add 1 tablespoon butter and leeks. Cook slowly for 2 - 5 minutes; leeks should remain crisp. Adjust seasonings and set aside warm.
Assembly:
---------
Remove filet of rabbit from saddle. Cut saddle bone crosswise into two portions; place on plates. Surround each with leeks.
Slice filet thinly lengthwise; fan slices over leeks. Cover with sauce, garnish with rosemary sprigs. Serve immediately.
